I want to create an app that acts as a ‘one stop shop’ for people transacting on real estate in Victoria, Australia to help them manage and plan their affairs. The app intends to demystify what’s involved with property transactions. 

The planned slogan is “Real Estate Doesn’t Have to be Overwhelming”.

The planned app name is ‘PropertyGuru’

There are myriad fixed and variable expenses associated with transacting on real estate. For a buyer, fees include:
•	Land transfer fees (Calculator: https://www.land.vic.gov.au/land-registration/fees-guides-and-forms/transfer-of-land-fees-calculator)
•	Other typical fees paid include registration of mortgage and lodgement of caveat which are outlined here: https://www.land.vic.gov.au/land-registration/fees-guides-and-forms
•	Land Transfer (Stamp) Duty (calculator: https://www.e-business.sro.vic.gov.au/calculators/land-transfer-duty) 
•	Legal fees
•	Land transfer duty is often dependant on whether or not a buyer is a first home owner or is the holder of an eligible concession card. There should be specific parts of the app that allow users to select any of these particular eligibilities to ensure their projected duty is accurate

For a vendor (seller) fees include:
•	Real estate agent commission
•	Advertising
•	Legal fees
•	Discharge of the balance of the mortgage

A feature of the app will be the ability for the enter to easily enter this data into fields so that they can instantly see a ‘bottom line’ of how much the transaction is going to cost them.

A function of the app will be ‘AuctionGuru’ where a party can easily enter their current bid (perhaps via a sliding scale which can easily have its increments adjusted, as the bidding increments will depend on how the auction is being run) which simply translates to a ‘bottom line’ in real time so that they can see the true cost of their purchase price (for a buyer) or how much money they can expect to walk away with after the deduction of all fees (for a vendor).

Another calculator for a vendor will be a ‘reverse calculator’, where they can the minimum sum of money they want to walk away with at settlement and what actual sale price this translates to.

There will also be an information section of the app that is basically a fancy ‘FAQ’ section. This might be based on questions being input or having an easily navigated contents (or both) to ensure that this app is the preferred source of information about property transactions rather than a web search or LLM search. The content will be original (not LLM) and be associated with my particular style which will resonate throughout the app.

Real estate agent referrals are a source of business for conveyancers. I will promote this app to agents and will give them a code to pass on to their clients which will allow them to download it for free. Each agent will be given a unique code. When this code is used to download the app for free the agent’s photo and contact details will appear on the home screen. The app will make it clear that the agent doesn’t endorse the app, but acts as a reminder of the agent’s ‘generosity’ by giving them a free download code to access the app.

The app will be listed for a download fee on the app stores to create a perception of generosity for the agents’ clients. The app will have subtle links and references to my conveyancing business throughout. The overall idea is to funnel work into my conveyancing firm via the app with subtle references to my firm.

Okay, I understand. You want to create a React Native + Expo app called "PropertyGuru" to help people in Victoria, Australia, manage and plan their real estate transactions. The app should demystify the process and act as a one-stop shop for calculating costs, providing information, and subtly promoting your conveyancing business. You also want to incorporate a referral system for real estate agents.

Here's a plan for the first version of the app:

**Features for the First Version:**

*   **Cost Calculators:** Implement calculators for both buyers and vendors, including:
    *   Land transfer fees (using the provided calculator link).
    *   Land transfer duty (using the provided calculator link), with options for first home owners and concession card holders.
    *   Other fees like registration of mortgage, lodgement of caveat, legal fees, real estate agent commission, advertising, and discharge of mortgage.
*   **AuctionGuru:** A real-time bidding calculator that shows the "bottom line" cost for buyers and net proceeds for vendors, with an adjustable sliding scale for bid increments.
*   **Reverse Calculator:** For vendors, allowing them to input their desired net proceeds and calculate the required sale price.
*   **FAQ/Information Section:** A comprehensive and easily navigable section with original content about property transactions in Victoria.
*   **Agent Referral System:**
    *   Allow users to enter a unique agent code.
    *   Display the agent's photo and contact details on the home screen if a valid code is entered.
    *   Include a disclaimer stating that the agent doesn't endorse the app.

**Design Elements:**

*   **Colors:**
    *   Background: `#f9f9f9` (Light grey)
    *   Text: `#212121` (Dark grey)
    *   TextSecondary: `#757575` (Medium grey)
    *   Primary: `#3f51b5` (Indigo)
    *   Secondary: `#f44336` (Red)
    *   Accent: `#ffca28` (Amber)
    *   Card: `#ffffff` (White)
    *   Highlight: `#e8eaf6` (Light indigo)
*   **Typography:** Use modern, clean fonts like "System" for a native feel.

Now, let's create a plan for the code changes.

1.  **Project Setup:** No changes needed here, assuming the project is already set up with React Native and Expo.
2.  **Create Core Components:**
    *   Create a `Calculator` component that will house the different calculators.
    *   Create individual calculator components for Land Transfer Fees, Land Transfer Duty, AuctionGuru, and Reverse Calculator.
    *   Create an `FAQ` component to display the information section.
    *   Create an `AgentReferral` component to handle the agent code and display agent information.
3.  **Modify `app/(tabs)/(home)/index.tsx`:**
    *   Add the `AgentReferral` component to the home screen.
    *   Add navigation elements to access the `Calculator` and `FAQ` components.
4.  **Implement Calculators:**
    *   Implement the logic for each calculator based on the formulas and information provided in the user's request.
    *   Use state management (e.g., useState) to handle user input and update the calculated results.
5.  **Implement FAQ Section:**
    *   Create a data structure to store the FAQ content.
    *   Display the FAQ content in an easily navigable format.
6.  **Implement Agent Referral System:**
    *   Store the agent code in the app's state.
    *   Fetch agent information based on the entered code (this might require a simple data structure or a more complex backend solution).
    *   Display the agent's photo and contact details on the home screen.

After the coding agent implements the plan, the `modalDemos` array from `app/(tabs)/(home)/index.tsx` should be removed.

Item 	Dutiable value of the dutiable property 	Rate of duty 
1 	Not more than $25 000 	1 ⋅ 4% of the dutiable value
